title: Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve
tagline: Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve — wildlife reserve of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve is located in the South Western Ghats montane rain forests in Tirunelveli district and Kanyakumari district in the South India.
overview.description: Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve is located in the South Western Ghats montane rain forests in Tirunelveli district and Kanyakumari district in the South India. It is the second-largest protected area in Tamil Nadu and part of Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Servalar dam
topPlaces.description: The Servalar Dam was set up for power generation in 1986, with a capacity of 1225 million cubic feet. The peak water level of the dam is 156 ft.

topPlaces.name: Vikramasingapuram
topPlaces.description: Vickramasingapuram or V.K.Puram is a Town in Tirunelveli District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It falls under the Ambasamudram Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Agasthiyar Falls
topPlaces.description: Agasthiyar Falls (also called Papanasam Falls) is located close to Papanasam in Tirunelveli district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The falls is located on the Western Ghats and to the south of Papanasam lower, the lake from where Tamiraparani River originates.

topPlaces.name: A & F Harvey Brothers
topPlaces.description: A & F Harvey Brothers, first Spinning Cotton Mill, established by Scottish brothers Andrew Harvey and Frank Harvey, in the year 1880.

topPlaces.name: Manimutharu
topPlaces.description: Manimutharu is a panchayat town in Tirunelveli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Papanasam Dam
topPlaces.description: The Papanasam dam also known as Karaiyar Dam is located 49 kilometres (30 mi) away from Tirunelveli in Tamil Nadu, India. The dam is used to irrigate 86,107 acres (34,846 ha) of paddy fields in Tirunelveli and Tuticorin districts.

topPlaces.name: Papanasam, Tirunelveli
topPlaces.description: Papanasam also spelt as Pavanasam is a famous picnic spot in Tirunelveli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It falls under the Ambasamudram Taluk.

items.note: The Servalar Dam was set up for power generation in 1986, with a capacity of 1225 million cubic feet. The peak water level of the dam is 156 ft.
Vickramasingapuram or V.K.Puram is a Town in Tirunelveli District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It falls under the Ambasamudram Taluk.

Agasthiyar Falls (also called Papanasam Falls) is located close to Papanasam in Tirunelveli district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The falls is located on the Western Ghats and to the south of Papanasam lower, the lake from where Tamiraparani River originates.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (49 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (46 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: Stays in Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kalakkad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The top attractions are Servalar dam, Vikramasingapuram, Agasthiyar Falls, A & F Harvey Brothers, Manimutharu.
